channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Counting is underway in Ireland's election as 3 parties battle for top place
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — A marathon vote-counting exercise was underway Saturday in Ireland’s national election after an exit poll suggested that the contest is a close-fought race among the country’s three largest political parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump wants pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner to become US ambassador to France
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday he intends to nominate real estate developer Charles Kushner, father of Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, to serve as ambassador to France.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania restaurant ordered to pay $184,000 for withholding tips
item.description: PITTSBURGH, Pa. (WHTM) - A Pennsylvania restaurant was ordered to pay $184,000 in back wages, damages, and penalties after an investigation found they withheld tips. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, ABC 4

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, ABC 4

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KRON4

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: (WGHP) — Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, ABC 4

item.title: Why your favorite catalogs are smaller this holiday season
item.description: While retailers hope to go big this holiday season, customers may notice that the catalogs arriving in their mailboxes are smaller.
item.dc:creator: David Sharp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the plastic pollution crisis as treaty talks conclude in South Korea
item.description: The world’s nations will wrap up negotiating a treaty this weekend to address the global crisis of plastic pollution.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Tennessee head coach Bill Battle dies at 82
item.description: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (WATE) -- Former University of Tennessee Coach William "Bill" Battle III died on Thanksgiving morning at the age of 82, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Lake-effect snow heading for New York state could disrupt travel and football
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — Black Friday was giving way to a white weekend in parts of New York state, with the first big snow of the season threatening to bury towns along lakes Erie and Ontario during a hectic holiday travel and shopping weekend.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The ‘Most Wished For’ Amazon items on sale for Black Friday
item.description: These are the most-wanted Amazon items among shoppers — at least, the ones that have great deals going on for Black Friday
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Head of Ohio's teacher pension fund retires Sunday, saying fund is strong despite staff vacancies
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The outgoing head of Ohio's troubled teachers retirement system said that she is leaving the fund in strong fiscal condition, despite the turmoil at the top that her successor will inherit.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auto industry may lose 17 percent of profits due to Trump’s tariff plan: Report
item.description: Carmakers in the U.S. and Europe could lose up to 17 percent of their combined annual core profits in a worst-case scenario if the U.S. imposes steep tariffs on Europe, and two key U.S. allies, Mexico and Canada, a report from S&P Global released Friday showed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 tips to avoid overspending this holiday season
item.description: Nearly 30% of credit card users are still paying off last year's holiday debt. Here are five tips to avoid overspending.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: US faith-based missionaries build homes for Mexican poor south of the border
item.description: A group of 150 faith-based volunteers from North Texas traveled south of the border in the days before Thanksgiving and built several homes for the poor in Reynosa, Mexico. One family sat down with Border Report and detailed their experience.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Jon Batiste, Ledisi, Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle to perform during Super Bowl pregame
item.description: The Super Bowl pregame will have some Louisiana flavor: Multi-talented performer Jon Batiste will hit the stage to sing the national anthem, while Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle are slated to perform “America the Beautiful.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Arizona executions to resume, breaking 2-year pause during review of state death penalty procedures
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Executions will resume in Arizona following a two-year pause, the state's top prosecutor says.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Northeast hit with rain, snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities
item.description: The U.S. Northeast has been hit with a winter storm that has brought rain and snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities and disrupted travel plans for those moving around on the holiday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Is your turkey dry? This might be why
item.description: Every year in the weeks leading up to Thanksgiving, the holiday conversation inevitably turns to one question regarding the annual feast: is turkey any good? While debates and discussions about which side dishes are the best, it seems like the star of the show often gets overlooked, or worse, malign
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Amazon workers on strike from Black Friday to Cyber Monday
item.description: (WFXR) - Amazons workers across 20 countries, including the United States, are striking against what the organizing labor union calls anti-worker and anti-democratic practices.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Jaxtheimer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs denied bail for a third time
item.description: He was denied bail in September and earlier this month when the judge deemed Combs a flight risk because of his money and connections.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Travelers who waited to make Thanksgiving trips are hitting the biggest crowds so far
item.description: Travelers who waited until the last day to make their Thanksgiving holiday treks need to be prepared for busy highways and the most crowded day yet this week at the nation's airports.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge finds New York City in contempt over jail conditions, moves closer to a federal takeover
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge on Wednesday found New York City in contempt for failing to staunch violence and brutality at its jails, a scathing ruling that puts the troubled Rikers Island jail complex on the verge of a federal takeover. In a written decision, U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This pyramid in Utah is a winery, a temple, and home to the mummy of a religious leader
item.description: Did you know? There's a pyramid in Salt Lake City that is a winery, a temple, and the home of the Summum religion.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers' resentencing request hearing pushed back to January
item.description: The Menendez brothers have waiting nearly three decades, and now they'll have to wait a little longer to hear their fates.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4
